supporting_works.title: The pharmacopoeia universalis or, complete encyclopedia of the materia medica; contained in the pharmacopoeias of London, Edinburgh, and Dublin as well as of all those of Europe and America ; and of the dispensatories, formularies and chemical works ... With an appendix ... / by A. J. L. Jourdan : Edited by J. Rennie.

A treatise on domestic medicine : pointing out, in plain language, and as free from professional terms as possible, the nature, symptoms, causes, probable terminations, and treatment of all diseases incident to men, women, and children, in both cold and warm climates : as also, appropriate prescriptions in English, and the doses of medicine which are suitable to different ages : including likewise effectual means for preventing the extension of all infectious diseases, and annihilating the power of every kind of contagion, and rules for enabling Europeans who visit a warm climate to escape the yellow fever, and long enjoy a good state of health
supporting_works.creator: Thomas, Robert, 1753-1835.

supporting_works.title: Modern domestic medicine : being a treatise divested of professional terms on the nature, causes, symptoms, and treatment of the diseases of men, women, and children, in both cold and warm climates : with appropriate prescriptions in English : the whole preceded by practical rules for the preservation of health, the best means for invigorating the human body and prolonging life, and the mode of preventing the extension of infectious disorders, as also of annihilating contagion
supporting_works.creator: Thomas, Robert, 1753-1835.

retrieval_text: Cascarilla is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 263 works and 721 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Cascarilla. Common plant-part co-mentions include Bark, Root, Seed, Flower, Leaf, Resin. Common preparation co-mentions include Infusion, Tincture, Powder, Oil, Extract, Pill. Common condition co-mentions include Fever, Cold or catarrh, Diarrhoea or looseness, Gout, Vomiting or nausea, Dysentery. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Child-use caution, Bleeding caution, Toxicity watch, Allergy or sensitivity caution.
